chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 – The woman performs Hajj on behalf of the man

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/217)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/217)
text: هُ قَالَ تَحُجُّ الْمَرْأَةُ عَنْ أَخِيهَا وَ عَنْ أُخْتِهَا وَ قَالَ تَحُجُّ الْمَرْأَةُ عَنِ ابْنِهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Rafa’at,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The woman can perform Hajj on behalf of her brother and on behalf of her sister’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The woman can perform Hajj on behalf of her son’.
en.sarwar: 4. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah ibn Ayyub from Rifa’ah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is permissible for a woman to perform al-Hajj for her brother, and sister.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘She can perform al-Hajj for her son also.’”
